 {"id":"bd-20q09","title":"Integration: Support graceful swarm shutdown","description":"## Purpose
Connect AutoRespawner to PromptInjector to automatically inject marching orders after an agent is respawned, maintaining workflow continuity.

## Implementation Details

```go
// RespawnPromptCoordinator ensures prompts are injected after respawn
type RespawnPromptCoordinator struct {
    respawner      *AutoRespawner
    injector       *PromptInjector
    planStore      *SwarmPlanStore  // Stores active plan for prompt lookup
    injectionDelay time.Duration    // Wait for agent to initialize
    logger         *slog.Logger
}

// OnRespawnComplete is called by AutoRespawner after successful respawn
func (c *RespawnPromptCoordinator) OnRespawnComplete(event RespawnCompleteEvent) error {
    c.logger.Info("respawn complete, preparing prompt injection",
        "pane", event.SessionPane,
        "agent_type", event.AgentType,
        "project", event.Project)
    
    // Wait for agent to initialize
    c.logger.Debug("waiting for agent initialization",
        "delay", c.injectionDelay)
    time.Sleep(c.injectionDelay)
    
    // Get the prompt for this pane from the active plan
    prompt := c.planStore.GetPromptForPane(event.SessionPane)
    if prompt == "" {
        // Use default prompt based on project
        prompt = c.generateDefaultPrompt(event)
    }
    
    // Inject the prompt
    paneSpec := PaneSpec{
        Index:     event.PaneIndex,
        Project:   event.Project,
        AgentType: event.AgentType,
    }
    
    if err := c.injector.InjectWithQuirks(event.SessionPane, paneSpec, prompt); err != nil {
        c.logger.Error("post-respawn prompt injection failed",
            "pane", event.SessionPane,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("inject after respawn: %w", err)
    }
    
    c.logger.Info("post-respawn prompt injected",
        "pane", event.SessionPane,
        "prompt_len", len(prompt))
    
    return nil
}

func (c *RespawnPromptCoordinator) generateDefaultPrompt(event RespawnCompleteEvent) string {
    return fmt.Sprintf(`You were respawned after hitting rate limits. 
Continue working on project: %s
Resume where you left off or check the project's open beads for tasks.`, 
        filepath.Base(event.Project))
}

// RespawnCompleteEvent is emitted by AutoRespawner
type RespawnCompleteEvent struct {
    SessionPane string
    PaneIndex   int
    AgentType   string
    Project     string
    RespawnTime time.Time
}
```

## Callback Registration
```go
// In swarm initialization
respawner := NewAutoRespawner(...)
coordinator := NewRespawnPromptCoordinator(respawner, injector, planStore, logger)

// Register callback
respawner.OnComplete(coordinator.OnRespawnComplete)
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log respawn completion receipt
- Log initialization delay
- Log prompt source (plan vs default)
- Log injection success/failure

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Callback triggered on respawn completion
- [ ] Configurable initialization delay
- [ ] Prompt lookup from active plan
- [ ] Fallback default prompt generation
- [ ] Uses PromptInjector with agent quirks
- [ ] Error handling without blocking respawner","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:40.886893948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:29:13.480989225Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T05:29:13.480848943Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20q09","depends_on_id":"bd-3cdsi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-20q09","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-214u5","title":"Task: Integrate audit logging throughout NTM codebase","description":"## Overview
Add audit log calls at all significant action points in NTM.

## Deliverables
1. Integration points:

   CLI layer (internal/cli/):
   - Log every command execution
   - Include command args (sanitize secrets)
   - Log command result (success/failure)

   Session management (internal/session/):
   - Session creation with config
   - Session termination with reason
   - Agent spawn events

   Message sending (internal/agent/):
   - Prompts sent (preview, not full content)
   - Response received (summary)
   - Error conditions

   State changes:
   - Agent state transitions
   - Configuration changes
   - Resource allocation/release

2. Payload guidelines:
   - Never log full prompts (could be huge)
   - Include preview (first 100 chars)
   - Include length, token count estimates
   - Sanitize secrets/API keys

3. Correlation:
   - Generate correlation_id at command start
   - Thread through all related log entries
   - Enable tracing request flow

4. Performance considerations:
   - Async logging (don't block operations)
   - Batch writes for efficiency
   - Skip logging in test mode (configurable)

## Implementation Notes
- Create helper functions for common patterns
- Use structured logging consistently
- Consider log levels (debug, info, warn, error)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All significant actions logged
- Correlation IDs enable request tracing
- No sensitive data in logs
- Logging doesn't impact performance noticeably
- Unit tests verify logging at key points","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:42.660614881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:59:09.279662706Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:59:09.279579681Z","close_reason":"Completed audit logging integration across CLI/session/robot/agent; tests pass","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-214u5","depends_on_id":"bd-3fif","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-214u5","depends_on_id":"bd-3pbe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2lsyy","title":"caut Integration: Usage Tracking and Quota Visibility","description":"Integrate caut (coding_agent_usage_tracker) for real-time quota and spend tracking across all agents.

## Project Location
/dp/coding_agent_usage_tracker (Rust CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Without visibility into API usage:
- Surprise bills at end of month
- Agents hit limits unexpectedly
- No way to budget across projects
- Can't see which agents are most expensive

## What caut Does

caut monitors LLM provider usage across 16+ providers:
- Claude (claude.ai, API)
- OpenAI (ChatGPT, API)
- Gemini (web, API)
- Anthropic Console
- Many more

### Data Sources
- Browser extension intercepts
- API response headers
- CLI tool output parsing
- Provider dashboard scraping

### Key caut Commands
```bash
caut status              # Current usage summary
caut status --json       # JSON output for agents
caut history --today     # Today's usage breakdown
caut cost --month        # Monthly cost projection
caut alerts set 80       # Alert at 80% of limits
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### 1. Dashboard Widget
Main use case: see usage at a glance in NTM dashboard
- Per-provider usage bars
- Cost accumulator (daily/monthly)
- Alert indicators when approaching limits

### 2. Robot Mode Commands
- --robot-quota-status: Get all quota info as JSON
- --robot-quota-check <provider>: Check specific provider

### 3. Proactive Alerts
When caut reports >80% usage:
- Highlight in dashboard (red/yellow)
- Consider triggering CAAM rotation if enabled

### 4. Per-Agent Attribution
If possible, tag API calls with agent/pane ID for:
- Per-agent cost breakdown
- Identify expensive agents
- Budget allocation

## Considerations
- caut runs independently, NTM just reads its output
- Polling frequency: every 60 seconds
- caut may have stale data (depends on data sources)
- Don't duplicate alerting if CAAM also tracking","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:56:25.641626929Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:31:07.296802539Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:31:07.296753487Z","close_reason":"All caut integration tasks complete: polling, robot commands, dashboard widget, CAAM coordinator","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lsyy","depends_on_id":"bd-mk1i5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2mgl","title":"Unit Tests: Context monitoring and injection","description":"## Comprehensive Unit Test Suite for Proactive Context Management

### Test Coverage Required
1. **ContextMonitor tests** (internal/context/monitor_test.go)
   - Context length estimation
   - Threshold detection (50%, 75%, 90%)
   - Alert trigger logic
   - Debouncing behavior

2. **ContextInjector tests** (internal/context/inject_test.go)
   - Summary injection formatting
   - Key facts extraction
   - Decision preservation
   - Token budget constraints

3. **ContextEstimator tests** (internal/context/estimate_test.go)
   - Token counting accuracy
   - Multi-model estimation (Claude vs GPT)
   - Scrollback parsing

### Test Data
- Sample scrollbacks at various lengths

-{"id":"bd-h3ha","title":"Add ntm send --randomize - randomize order of individualized prompts to avoid thundering herd on same files","description":"## Overview
Add --randomize flag to ntm send to randomize the order of individualized prompts, avoiding thundering herd problems when multiple agents start working on overlapping files.

## Problem
When sending individualized prompts (e.g., each agent gets a different bead), agents often start simultaneously on related files. This causes:
- Git conflicts when multiple agents edit the same area
- File reservation conflicts in Agent Mail
- Wasted work from merge conflicts

## Solution
`ntm send myproject --randomize` shuffles the order of prompts before sending.

## Implementation
```go
func randomizePrompts(prompts []Prompt) []Prompt {
    shuffled := make([]Prompt, len(prompts))
    copy(shuffled, prompts)
    rand.Shuffle(len(shuffled), func(i, j int) {
        shuffled[i], shuffled[j] = shuffled[j], shuffled[i]
    })
    return shuffled
}
```

## CLI Examples
```bash
# Randomize prompt order
ntm send myproject --randomize

# Combine with batch
ntm send myproject --batch prompts.txt --randomize

# Combine with delay for extra staggering  
ntm send myproject --randomize --delay 2s
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prompts shuffled before sending
- [ ] Deterministic seed option for reproducibility (--seed=N)
- [ ] Works with --batch and --distribute
- [ ] Logging shows shuffle order
- [ ] No effect if only one prompt

- Unit test: Seed produces reproducible order

## Test Script Structure

```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# scripts/test_swarm_e2e.sh
# End-to-end test for weighted multi-project agent swarm orchestration
#
# Prerequisites:
# - ntm binary built and in PATH
# - tmux installed
# - br (beads_rust) installed
# - Test project directories set up

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# Configuration
# ============================================================================
TEST_DIR="${TEST_DIR:-/tmp/ntm_e2e_test_$$}"
LOG_FILE="${TEST_DIR}/e2e_test.log"
VERBOSE="${VERBOSE:-1}"

# Colors for output
RED="\033[0;31m"
GREEN="\033[0;32m"
YELLOW="\033[1;33m"
NC="\033[0m" # No Color

# ============================================================================
# Logging Functions
# ============================================================================
log() {
    local level=$1
    shift
    local msg="$*"
    local timestamp=$(date "+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S.%3N")
    echo -e "[$timestamp] [$level] $msg" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_info()  { log "INFO " "$@"; }
log_warn()  { log "WARN " "${YELLOW}$*${NC}"; }
log_error() { log "ERROR" "${RED}$*${NC}"; }
log_pass()  { log "PASS " "${GREEN}$*${NC}"; }
log_fail()  { log "FAIL " "${RED}$*${NC}"; }

log_section() {
    echo "" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "============================================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "  $1" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    echo "============================================================" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
}

# ============================================================================
# Setup Functions
# ============================================================================
setup_test_environment() {
    log_section "Setting up test environment"
    
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR"
    log_info "Test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    log_info "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
    
    # Create test projects with different bead counts
    log_info "Creating test projects..."
    
    # Tier 1 project (400+ beads)
    create_test_project "tier1-project" 450
    
    # Tier 2 project (100-399 beads)
    create_test_project "tier2-project" 200
    
    # Tier 3 project (<100 beads)
    create_test_project "tier3-project" 50
    
    # Project without beads (should be skipped)
    mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/no-beads-project"
    log_info "Created no-beads-project (should be skipped)"
    
    log_pass "Test environment setup complete"
}

create_test_project() {
    local name=$1
    local bead_count=$2
    local project_dir="$TEST_DIR/$name"
    
    mkdir -p "$project_dir/.beads"
    
    # Create mock beads
    for i in $(seq 1 $bead_count); do
        echo "{\"id\":\"bd-$i\",\"status\":\"open\",\"title\":\"Test bead $i\"}" >> "$project_dir/.beads/issues.jsonl"
    done
    
    log_info "Created $name with $bead_count beads"
}

# ============================================================================
# Test Cases
# ============================================================================
test_dry_run() {
    log_section "Test: Dry Run Mode"
    
    log_info "Running: ntm swarm --scan-dir $TEST_DIR --dry-run"
    
    local output
    output=$(ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run 2>&1) || {
        log_fail "Dry run command failed"
        log_error "Output: $output"
        return 1
    }
    
    log_info "Dry run output:"
    echo "$output" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Verify expected output
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "tier1-project"; then
        log_pass "Tier 1 project detected"
    else
        log_fail "Tier 1 project NOT detected"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "tier2-project"; then
        log_pass "Tier 2 project detected"
    else
        log_fail "Tier 2 project NOT detected"
        return 1
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "no-beads-project"; then
        log_fail "No-beads project should NOT appear"
        return 1
    else
        log_pass "No-beads project correctly excluded"
    fi
    
    if echo "$output" | grep -q "Dry-run complete"; then
        log_pass "Dry run completed without execution"
    else
        log_fail "Dry run indicator not found"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Dry run test PASSED"
}

test_plan_output() {
    log_section "Test: Plan JSON Output"
    
    local plan_file="$TEST_DIR/swarm_plan.json"
    
    log_info "Running: ntm swarm --scan-dir $TEST_DIR --dry-run --output $plan_file"
    
    ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run --output "$plan_file" 2>&1 | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    
    if [[ \! -f "$plan_file" ]]; then
        log_fail "Plan file not created"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_info "Plan file created: $plan_file"
    log_info "Plan contents:"
    cat "$plan_file" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"
    
    # Validate JSON structure
    if \! jq -e . "$plan_file" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_fail "Plan file is not valid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    log_pass "Plan file is valid JSON"
    
    # Check required fields
    local total_agents
    total_agents=$(jq -r ".total_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Total agents in plan: $total_agents"
    
    if [[ "$total_agents" -gt 0 ]]; then
        log_pass "Plan contains agent allocations"
    else
        log_fail "Plan has no agent allocations"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Plan output test PASSED"
}

test_session_creation() {
    log_section "Test: Session Creation (requires tmux)"
    
    # Skip if tmux not available
    if \! command -v tmux &> /dev/null; then
        log_warn "tmux not available, skipping session creation test"
        return 0
    fi
    
    # Clean up any existing test sessions
    tmux kill-session -t "cc_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    log_info "Running swarm with single session for test..."
    log_info "ntm swarm --scan-dir $TEST_DIR --sessions-per-type 1 --dry-run"
    
    # For actual execution (commented out for safety):
    # ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --sessions-per-type 1
    
    # Verify sessions would be created
    local plan_file="$TEST_DIR/session_test_plan.json"
    ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --sessions-per-type 1 --dry-run --output "$plan_file"
    
    local session_count
    session_count=$(jq -r ".sessions | length" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Sessions in plan: $session_count"
    
    if [[ "$session_count" -ge 3 ]]; then
        log_pass "Plan includes expected session count (3 types)"
    else
        log_fail "Unexpected session count: $session_count"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Check session naming
    local cc_session
    cc_session=$(jq -r ".sessions[] | select(.agent_type==\"cc\") | .name" "$plan_file" | head -1)
    log_info "CC session name: $cc_session"
    
    if [[ "$cc_session" == "cc_agents_1" ]]; then
        log_pass "Session naming is correct"
    else
        log_fail "Session naming mismatch: expected cc_agents_1, got $cc_session"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Session creation test PASSED"
}

test_allocation_tiers() {
    log_section "Test: Allocation Tier Calculations"
    
    local plan_file="$TEST_DIR/tier_test_plan.json"
    ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run --output "$plan_file"
    
    # Check tier 1 allocation
    local tier1_cc
    tier1_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier1-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 1 (450 beads) CC allocation: $tier1_cc"
    
    # Check tier 2 allocation  
    local tier2_cc
    tier2_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier2-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 2 (200 beads) CC allocation: $tier2_cc"
    
    # Check tier 3 allocation
    local tier3_cc
    tier3_cc=$(jq -r ".allocations[] | select(.project.name==\"tier3-project\") | .cc_agents" "$plan_file")
    log_info "Tier 3 (50 beads) CC allocation: $tier3_cc"
    
    # Verify tier hierarchy
    if [[ "$tier1_cc" -gt "$tier2_cc" ]] && [[ "$tier2_cc" -ge "$tier3_cc" ]]; then
        log_pass "Tier allocation hierarchy is correct"
    else
        log_fail "Tier allocation hierarchy violated: T1=$tier1_cc, T2=$tier2_cc, T3=$tier3_cc"
        return 1
    fi
    
    log_pass "Allocation tier test PASSED"
}

# ============================================================================
# Cleanup
# ============================================================================
cleanup() {
    log_section "Cleanup"
    
    # Kill any test tmux sessions
    tmux kill-session -t "cc_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    tmux kill-session -t "cod_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    tmux kill-session -t "gmi_agents_1" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    if [[ "${KEEP_TEST_DIR:-0}" == "0" ]]; then
        log_info "Removing test directory: $TEST_DIR"
        rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
    else
        log_info "Keeping test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    fi
    
    log_info "Cleanup complete"
}

# ============================================================================
# Main
# ============================================================================
main() {
    log_section "NTM Swarm E2E Test Suite"
    log_info "Starting at $(date)"
    log_info "Test directory: $TEST_DIR"
    
    trap cleanup EXIT
    
    local failed=0
    
    setup_test_environment
    
    test_dry_run || ((failed++))
    test_plan_output || ((failed++))
    test_allocation_tiers || ((failed++))
    test_session_creation || ((failed++))
    
    log_section "Test Summary"
    if [[ $failed -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_pass "All tests PASSED"
        exit 0
    else
        log_fail "$failed test(s) FAILED"
        exit 1
    fi
}

main "$@"
```

+package palette
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"fmt"
+	"strings"
+
+	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/key"
+	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
+	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
+	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/config"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tools"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tui/theme"
+)
+
+// XFSearchResultsMsg carries xf search results back to the model.
+type XFSearchResultsMsg struct {
+	Query   string
+	Results []tools.XFSearchResult
+	Err     error
+}
+
+// xfSearchCmd runs xf search as a Bubble Tea command.
+func xfSearchCmd(query string, limit int) tea.Cmd {
+	return func() tea.Msg {
+		adapter := tools.NewXFAdapter()
+		if _, installed := adapter.Detect(); !installed {
+			return XFSearchResultsMsg{
+				Query: query,
+				Err:   fmt.Errorf("xf is not installed — run 'brew install xf' or see https://github.com/xf-sh/xf"),
+			}
+		}
+		ctx := context.Background()
+		results, err := adapter.Search(ctx, query, limit)
+		return XFSearchResultsMsg{
+			Query:   query,
+			Results: results,
+			Err:     err,
+		}
+	}
+}
+
+// initXFQuery creates the xf search text input.
+func initXFQuery(t theme.Theme) textinput.Model {
+	ti := textinput.New()
+	ti.Placeholder = "Search X/Twitter archive..."
+	ti.CharLimit = 200
+	ti.Width = 40
+	ti.PromptStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Blue)
+	ti.TextStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Text)
+	ti.PlaceholderStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Overlay)
+	ti.Cursor.Style = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Pink)
+	return ti
+}
+
+// enterXFSearch transitions the model to the xf search phase.
+func (m *Model) enterXFSearch() {
+	m.phase = PhaseXFSearch
+	m.xfResults = nil
+	m.xfCursor = 0
+	m.xfSearching = false
+	m.xfErr = nil
+	m.filter.Blur()
+	if m.xfQuery.Value() == "" {
+		m.xfQuery = initXFQuery(m.theme)
+	}
+	m.xfQuery.Focus()
+}
+
+// updateXFSearchPhase handles input in the xf search query phase.
+func (m *Model) updateXFSearchPhase(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
+	switch {
+	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Quit):
+		m.quitting = true
+		return *m, tea.Quit
+
+	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Back):
+		m.phase = PhaseCommand
+		m.filter.Focus()
+		m.xfQuery.Blur()
+		return *m, nil
+
+	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Select):
+		query := strings.TrimSpace(m.xfQuery.Value())
+		if query == "" {
+			return *m, nil
+		}
+		m.xfSearching = true
+		m.xfErr = nil
+		return *m, xfSearchCmd(query, 20)
+
+	default:
+		var cmd tea.Cmd
+		m.xfQuery, cmd = m.xfQuery.Update(msg)
+		return *m, cmd
+	}
+}
+
+// updateXFResultsPhase handles input when viewing xf search results.
+func (m *Model) updateXFResultsPhase(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
+	switch {
+	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Quit):
+		m.quitting = true
+		return *m, tea.Quit
+
+	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Back):
+		m.phase = PhaseXFSearch
+		m.xfQuery.Focus()
+		return *m, nil
+
+	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Up):
+		if m.xfCursor > 0 {
+			m.xfCursor--
+		}
+
+	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Down):
+		if m.xfCursor < len(m.xfResults)-1 {
+			m.xfCursor++
+		}
+
+	case key.Matches(msg, keys.Select):
+		if len(m.xfResults) > 0 && m.xfCursor < len(m.xfResults) {
+			result := m.xfResults[m.xfCursor]
+			prompt := formatXFResultPrompt(result)
+			m.selected = &config.PaletteCmd{
+				Key:      "xf-result",
+				Label:    "XF Search Result",
+				Category: "xf",
+				Prompt:   prompt,
+			}
+			m.phase = PhaseTarget
+		}
+	}
+	return *m, nil
+}
+
+// formatXFResultPrompt formats an xf search result as a prompt to send to agents.
+func formatXFResultPrompt(r tools.XFSearchResult) string {
+	var b strings.Builder
+	b.WriteString("Here is a relevant tweet from X/Twitter archive")
+	if r.CreatedAt != "" {
+		b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(" (%s)", r.CreatedAt))
+	}
+	b.WriteString(":\n\n")
+	b.WriteString(r.Content)
+	if r.ID != "" {
+		b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf("\n\n[Tweet ID: %s", r.ID))
+		if r.Type != "" {
+			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(", Type: %s", r.Type))
+		}
+		if r.Score > 0 {
+			b.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(", Score: %.2f", r.Score))
+		}
+		b.WriteString("]")
+	}
+	return b.String()
+}
+
+// viewXFSearchPhase renders the xf search query input.
+func (m Model) viewXFSearchPhase() string {
+	t := m.theme
+	ic := m.icons
+	var b strings.Builder
+
+	// Header
+	headerStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(t.Blue).Padding(1, 2)
+	b.WriteString(headerStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s XF Archive Search", ic.Search)))
+	b.WriteString("\n\n")
+
+	// Search input
+	inputStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Padding(0, 2)
+	b.WriteString(inputStyle.Render(m.xfQuery.View()))
+	b.WriteString("\n\n")
+
+	if m.xfSearching {
+		spinnerStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Mauve).Padding(0, 2)
+		b.WriteString(spinnerStyle.Render("Searching..."))
+		b.WriteString("\n")
+	}
+
+	if m.xfErr != nil {
+		errStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Error).Padding(0, 2)
+		b.WriteString(errStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s %v", ic.Cross, m.xfErr)))
+		b.WriteString("\n")
+	}
+
+	// Help
+	helpStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext).Padding(1, 2)
+	b.WriteString(helpStyle.Render("enter: search  esc: back  ctrl+c: quit"))
+
+	return lipgloss.Place(m.width, m.height, lipgloss.Center, lipgloss.Center, b.String())
+}
+
+// viewXFResultsPhase renders xf search results.
+func (m Model) viewXFResultsPhase() string {
+	t := m.theme
+	ic := m.icons
+	var b strings.Builder
+
+	// Header with query
+	headerStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(t.Blue).Padding(1, 2)
+	queryStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Mauve).Italic(true)
+	b.WriteString(headerStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s Results for %s", ic.Search, queryStyle.Render(m.xfQuery.Value()))))
+	b.WriteString("\n\n")
+
+	if len(m.xfResults) == 0 {
+		emptyStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext).Padding(0, 2)
+		b.WriteString(emptyStyle.Render("No results found."))
+		b.WriteString("\n")
+	} else {
+		// Available height for results
+		maxResults := m.height - 10
+		if maxResults < 3 {
+			maxResults = 3
+		}
+
+		// Calculate scroll window
+		startIdx := 0
+		if m.xfCursor >= maxResults {
+			startIdx = m.xfCursor - maxResults + 1
+		}
+		endIdx := startIdx + maxResults
+		if endIdx > len(m.xfResults) {
+			endIdx = len(m.xfResults)
+		}
+
+		countStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext).Padding(0, 2)
+		b.WriteString(countStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d results", len(m.xfResults))))
+		b.WriteString("\n\n")
+
+		for i := startIdx; i < endIdx; i++ {
+			result := m.xfResults[i]
+			isCursor := i == m.xfCursor
+
+			// Truncate content for display
+			content := strings.ReplaceAll(result.Content, "\n", " ")
+			maxLen := m.width - 12
+			if maxLen < 30 {
+				maxLen = 30
+			}
+			if len(content) > maxLen {
+				content = content[:maxLen-3] + "..."
+			}
+
+			var line string
+			if isCursor {
+				cursorStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Pink).Bold(true)
+				contentStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Text)
+				metaStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext)
+				line = fmt.Sprintf("  %s %s %s",
+					cursorStyle.Render(ic.Pointer),
+					contentStyle.Render(content),
+					metaStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[%s]", result.CreatedAt)),
+				)
+			} else {
+				contentStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Overlay)
+				metaStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("240"))
+				line = fmt.Sprintf("    %s %s",
+					contentStyle.Render(content),
+					metaStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[%s]", result.CreatedAt)),
+				)
+			}
+			b.WriteString(line)
+			b.WriteString("\n")
+		}
+	}
+
+	b.WriteString("\n")
+
+	// Preview of selected result
+	if len(m.xfResults) > 0 && m.xfCursor < len(m.xfResults) {
+		result := m.xfResults[m.xfCursor]
+		previewStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
+			Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
+			BorderForeground(t.Surface1).
+			Padding(1, 2).
+			Width(m.width - 8)
+		previewContent := result.Content
+		if result.Type != "" {
+			previewContent += fmt.Sprintf("\n\nType: %s", result.Type)
+		}
+		if result.Score > 0 {
+			previewContent += fmt.Sprintf(" | Score: %.2f", result.Score)
+		}
+		b.WriteString(lipgloss.NewStyle().Padding(0, 2).Render(previewStyle.Render(previewContent)))
+		b.WriteString("\n")
+	}
+
+	// Help
+	helpStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(t.Subtext).Padding(1, 2)
+	b.WriteString(helpStyle.Render("enter: send to agent  ↑↓: navigate  esc: back  ctrl+c: quit"))
+
+	return b.String()
+}

+// Package robot provides machine-readable output for AI agents.
+// xf_search.go implements the --robot-xf-search command (bd-7ijsy).
+package robot
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"errors"
+	"fmt"
+	"strings"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tools"
+)
+
+// XFSearchOutput represents the output for --robot-xf-search.
+type XFSearchOutput struct {
+	RobotResponse
+	Query string            `json:"query"`
+	Mode  string            `json:"mode,omitempty"`
+	Sort  string            `json:"sort,omitempty"`
+	Count int               `json:"count"`
+	Hits  []tools.XFSearchResult `json:"hits"`
+}
+
+// XFSearchOptions configures the GetXFSearch operation.
+type XFSearchOptions struct {
+	Query string
+	Limit int
+	Mode  string
+	Sort  string
+}
+
+// GetXFSearch searches the XF archive and returns structured output.
+// This function returns the data struct directly, enabling CLI/REST parity.
+func GetXFSearch(opts XFSearchOptions) (*XFSearchOutput, error) {
+	query := strings.TrimSpace(opts.Query)
+	output := &XFSearchOutput{
+		RobotResponse: NewRobotResponse(true),
+		Query:         query,
+		Mode:          opts.Mode,
+		Sort:          opts.Sort,
+	}
+
+	if query == "" {
+		output.RobotResponse = NewErrorResponse(
+			fmt.Errorf("query is required"),
+			ErrCodeInvalidFlag,
+			"Provide a search query, e.g., --robot-xf-search --query='error handling'",
+		)
+		return output, nil
+	}
+
+	adapter := tools.NewXFAdapter()
+	if _, installed := adapter.Detect(); !installed {
+		output.RobotResponse = NewErrorResponse(
+			fmt.Errorf("xf not installed"),
+			ErrCodeDependencyMissing,
+			"Install xf (X Find) and ensure it is on PATH",
+		)
+		return output, nil
+	}
+
+	limit := opts.Limit
+	if limit <= 0 {
+		limit = 20
+	}
+
+	ctx := context.Background()
+	results, err := adapter.Search(ctx, query, limit)
+	if err != nil {
+		code := ErrCodeInternalError
+		hint := "Try a different query or run xf doctor"
+		if errors.Is(err, tools.ErrTimeout) {
+			code = ErrCodeTimeout
+			hint = "XF search timed out; try a shorter query or reduce --limit"
+		}
+		output.RobotResponse = NewErrorResponse(err, code, hint)
+		return output, nil
+	}
+
+	if results == nil {
+		results = []tools.XFSearchResult{}
+	}
+	output.Hits = results
+	output.Count = len(results)
+
+	return output, nil
+}
+
+// PrintXFSearch outputs the XF search response as JSON/TOON.
+// This is a thin wrapper around GetXFSearch() for CLI output.
+func PrintXFSearch(opts XFSearchOptions) error {
+	output, err := GetXFSearch(opts)
+	if err != nil {
+		return err
+	}
+	return outputJSON(output)
+}
+
+// GetXFStatus returns XF tool health information.
+// This function returns the data struct directly, enabling CLI/REST parity.
+func GetXFStatus() (*XFStatusOutput, error) {
+	adapter := tools.NewXFAdapter()
+	output := &XFStatusOutput{
+		RobotResponse: NewRobotResponse(true),
+	}
+
+	path, installed := adapter.Detect()
+	output.XFAvailable = installed
+	if !installed {
+		output.RobotResponse = NewErrorResponse(
+			fmt.Errorf("xf not installed"),
+			ErrCodeDependencyMissing,
+			"Install xf (X Find) and ensure it is on PATH",
+		)
+		return output, nil
+	}
+
+	ctx := context.Background()
+	ver, err := adapter.Version(ctx)
+	if err == nil {
+		output.Version = ver.String()
+	}
+
+	health, err := adapter.Health(ctx)
+	if err == nil && health != nil {
+		output.Healthy = health.Healthy
+		output.Message = health.Message
+	}
+
+	_ = path // used only for detection
+	return output, nil
+}
+
+// XFStatusOutput represents the output for --robot-xf-status.
+type XFStatusOutput struct {
+	RobotResponse
+	XFAvailable bool   `json:"xf_available"`
+	Healthy     bool   `json:"healthy"`
+	Version     string `json:"version,omitempty"`
+	Message     string `json:"message,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// PrintXFStatus outputs the XF status response as JSON/TOON.
+// This is a thin wrapper around GetXFStatus() for CLI output.
+func PrintXFStatus() error {
+	output, err := GetXFStatus()
+	if err != nil {
+		return err
+	}
+	return outputJSON(output)
+}
+

+package e2e
+
+import (
+	"encoding/json"
+	"fmt"
+	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
+	"testing"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tmux"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/tests/testutil"
+)
+
+type batchSendOutput struct {
+	Success    bool     `json:"success"`
+	Session    string   `json:"session"`
+	Randomized bool     `json:"randomized"`
+	SeedUsed   int64    `json:"seed_used"`
+	Order      []string `json:"order"`
+}
+
+// Deterministic shuffle mirrors internal/cli/send.go logic for E2E verification.
+func expectedOrderSources(n int, seed int64) []string {
+	perm := make([]int, n)
+	for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
+		perm[i] = i
+	}
+	if n <= 1 {
+		return []string{"line:1"}
+	}
+	var x uint64 = uint64(seed)
+	if x == 0 {
+		x = 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15
+	}
+	next := func() uint64 {
+		x ^= x << 13
+		x ^= x >> 7
+		x ^= x << 17
+		return x
+	}
+	for i := n - 1; i > 0; i-- {
+		j := int(next() % uint64(i+1))
+		perm[i], perm[j] = perm[j], perm[i]
+	}
+
+	out := make([]string, 0, n)
+	for _, idx := range perm {
+		out = append(out, fmt.Sprintf("line:%d", idx+1))
+	}
+	return out
+}
+
+// bd-h3ha: E2E for --randomize + --seed in batch send mode (JSON output includes execution order).
+func TestSendBatchRandomizeWithSeed(t *testing.T) {
+	testutil.E2ETestPrecheckThrottled(t)
+
+	logger := testutil.NewTestLogger(t, t.TempDir())
+
+	// Isolate all history/state writes so this test never touches the user's real ~/.local/share or ~/.ntm.
+	t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir())
+	t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", t.TempDir())
+
+	sessionName := fmt.Sprintf("e2e_send_randomize_%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
+	projectsBase := t.TempDir()
+
+	configPath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "config.toml")
+	configContent := fmt.Sprintf(`
+projects_base = %q
+
+[agents]
+claude = "bash"
+`, projectsBase)
+	if err := os.WriteFile(configPath, []byte(configContent), 0644); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("failed to write test config: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	t.Cleanup(func() {
+		_ = tmux.KillSession(sessionName)
+	})
+
+	logger.LogSection("spawn session")
+	out, err := logger.Exec("ntm", "--config", configPath, "spawn", sessionName, "--cc=1", "--json")
+	logger.Log("spawn output: %s (err=%v)", string(out), err)
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("ntm spawn failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Give tmux time to create panes.
+	time.Sleep(750 * time.Millisecond)
+	testutil.AssertSessionExists(t, logger, sessionName)
+	testutil.AssertPaneCountAtLeast(t, logger, sessionName, 2) // user pane + 1 agent
+
+	// Create batch file.
+	batchPath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "batch.txt")
+	batchContent := "echo RAND_A\necho RAND_B\necho RAND_C\necho RAND_D\necho RAND_E\n"
+	if err := os.WriteFile(batchPath, []byte(batchContent), 0644); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("failed to write batch file: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	seed := int64(123)
+	logger.LogSection("send batch randomized (json)")
+	out, err = logger.Exec(
+		"ntm", "--config", configPath,
+		"send", sessionName,
+		"--batch", batchPath,
+		"--agent", "1",
+		"--randomize",
+		"--seed", fmt.Sprintf("%d", seed),
+		"--no-cass-check",
+		"--json",
+	)
+	logger.Log("send output: %s (err=%v)", string(out), err)
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("ntm send (batch randomized) failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	var got batchSendOutput
+	if err := json.Unmarshal(out, &got); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("failed to parse send JSON: %v\nraw=%s", err, string(out))
+	}
+	if !got.Success {
+		t.Fatalf("expected success=true, got false")
+	}
+	if !got.Randomized {
+		t.Fatalf("expected randomized=true, got false")
+	}
+	if got.SeedUsed != seed {
+		t.Fatalf("expected seed_used=%d, got %d", seed, got.SeedUsed)
+	}
+	if len(got.Order) != 5 {
+		t.Fatalf("expected order length 5, got %d (%v)", len(got.Order), got.Order)
+	}
+
+	wantOrder := expectedOrderSources(5, seed)
+	for i := range wantOrder {
+		if got.Order[i] != wantOrder[i] {
+			t.Fatalf("order[%d]=%q, want %q (got=%v)", i, got.Order[i], wantOrder[i], got.Order)
+		}
+	}
+}
